definition: A condition or supporting factor on which something else depends, arises, or is sustained.
notes: The project keeps condition as the default because it is broad enough for dependent-arising formulas and other causal analyses without falsely implying a single strong efficient cause. Support is acceptable when the enabling or sustaining side needs emphasis. Cause is discouraged as a blanket rendering because paccaya is wider than hetu and often names conditions that are concurrent, supportive, or structurally necessary rather than linear causes. Translators should keep linked -paccaya compounds and X-paccayā Y formulas aligned with this headword unless a compound explicitly records a narrower override.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and dependent-arising contexts
In phrases of the form X-paccayā Y
context_rules.notes: Use the standard English frame with X as condition, Y.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing enablement or sustenance
context_rules.notes: Allowed alternate when the line benefits from it.

context_rules.context: When distinguishing paccaya from hetu in analytical discussion
context_rules.notes: Useful where condition alone may sound too light or too generic.

example_phrases.translation: with ignorance as condition, putting together
with name-and-form as condition, the six sense fields
example_phrases.notes: Shows the same formula applied to another link.

example_phrases.translation: when this is present, this comes to be

version_notes: Expanded to distinguish paccaya from narrower cause-language and to encode standard formula handling for dependent-arising translation.
